danger_signals.visual_form: Jungle wildlife sprites (animated animals in side-scrolling environment)
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats to avoid or defeat; blocking progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinct enemy sprite appearing at level midpoints and endpoints
danger_signals.meaning: Major danger requiring projectile attacks to defeat; cannot be jumped on

danger_signals.visual_form: Central antagonist character placed in jungle environment
danger_signals.meaning: Primary threat driving narrative; Mowgli hunted and must reach human village to escape

danger_signals.visual_form: Six-minute timer per level (UI-based, not visual environment)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger of time constraint; failure to complete level within window

danger_signals.visual_form: Level geometry requiring jumping, climbing vines, navigating terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards; failed navigation leads to damage/death

safety_signals.visual_form: Bagheera, Baloo, King Louie, Kaa placed as discoverable characters in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe encounter; characters to locate after gem collection; narrative progression allies

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items scattered throughout levels (8-12 required per level)
safety_signals.meaning: Progress marker; accumulation signals readiness to find friendly character and advance

safety_signals.visual_form: Invincibility masks, coconuts, double banana shots, boomerang bananas as pickups
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety enhancement; banana projectile is base weapon; powerups amplify capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Narrative endpoint; goal state after defeating Shere Khan
safety_signals.meaning: Ultimate safety; successful escape from jungle threat

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jungle environment; travels through forests, vines, platforming terrain

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-class rendering; larger sprite than regular enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hunting throughout jungle; primary threat driving plot

faction_indicators.faction: Jungle allies (Bagheera, Baloo, King Louie, Kaa)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual character sprites from Disney film
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kaa noted as 'hypnotist snake' but treated as character to meet, not enemy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Placed within levels as discovery objectives; friendly encounter markers

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varied enemy types requiring projectile combat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout jungle levels; blocking platforming paths

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mowgli hunted and unsafe in jungle
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shere Khan present as antagonist; jungle wildlife enemies throughout levels; danger-heavy environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must avoid/defeat enemies; must collect gems to progress; must reach friendly characters; six-minute time pressure enforces urgency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gems scattered as collectible items (8-12 required depending on difficulty)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level incomplete until gem threshold reached; friendly character NPC cannot be accessed without gem collection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss character sprite appears at level midpoints and endpoints; larger than standard enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat required; projectile attacks only damage (jumping ineffective); defeat necessary to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Narrative progression through chapters; sequential stage descriptions advancing plot toward escape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Chapters sequentially structure gameplay; player advances through story stages toward village endpoint

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Invincibility masks, coconuts, double banana shots, boomerang bananas as pickup items
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced combat capability; temporary safety improvement; weapon variants available during play

environmental_storytelling.example: Each stage opens with description of story context and objectives, communicating plot progression from jungle danger toward village escape
Bagheera, Baloo, King Louie, Kaa positioned as discoverable NPCs within levels, embedding narrative allies as gameplay goals
Platforming terrain (vines, obstacles), wildlife enemies, and Shere Khan threat collectively establish jungle as dangerous zone requiring escape
Boss characters appearing every other level serve as story beats reinforcing escalating threat until final Shere Khan confrontation
Banana projectile as base weapon with powerup variants (coconuts, boomerangs) reflect Mowgli's jungle survival toolkit

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can run, jump, climb vines, shoot projectiles; must avoid enemies or defeat via weapons
Explores level terrain to accumulate required gem count (8-12 based on difficulty)
Seeks friendly character NPC after gem threshold reached; triggers level completion
Engage Mowgli; damage only from projectile attacks; jumping ineffective; defeat required to progress
Block platforming paths; require avoidance or projectile defeat; scattered throughout levels

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Friendly NPCs (Bagheera, Baloo, King Louie, Kaa)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Remain stationary within level as objectives; safe encounters; narrative progression triggers

notes: Wikipedia source provides narrative context and gameplay structure but limited detail on specific visual manifestation systems. The game uses platformer conventions (side-scrolling terrain, collectibles, timed progression) to communicate danger and story state. Visual communication relies heavily on character sprite presence (allies vs. antagonists), environmental platforming design, and collectible-gating mechanics. Boss encounters serve as narrative punctuation. The jungle setting itself functions as primary danger communicator—hostile terrain requiring navigation skill. Powerup system communicates temporary advantage states. Six-minute timer imposes narrative urgency around escape from Shere Khan threat. Chapter structure with opening descriptions provides explicit narrative framing that contexualizes visual/mechanical danger.
